stories.title: Garfield Twp planners back option to freeze new data centers
stories.dek: A township panel has advanced a moratorium proposal while residents debate the impact of large-scale facilities on local power and land use. The move comes as data-center interest grows in northwest Michigan. Grand Traverse County residents could see development paused if township leaders adopt the recommendation.
stories.source_title: Garfield Township panel recommends moratorium option amid data center debate - WPBN

stories.title: Saline Township data center swells to $16B with financing locked
stories.dek: Developers have secured funding that pushes the Saline project cost to $16 billion. Local taxpayers will feel the impact through any tax incentives and new power demand. The scale now rivals the largest U.S. campuses and will shape Washtenaw County growth for years.
stories.source_title: Saline Township data center price tag balloons to $16B as developers lock in financing - AOL.com

stories.title: Solon Township shelves rules meant to curb data centers
stories.dek: The board delayed an ordinance that would have restricted where large facilities could locate. Residents in the township now face continued uncertainty over future projects and their impact on local roads and power use. The pause keeps the status quo while officials weigh next steps.
stories.source_title: Solon Township board tables ordinance proposal to limit data centers - AOL.com

stories.title: Carpenters union throws support behind STREAM data center build
stories.dek: The union backing the STREAM project signals hundreds of construction jobs could come to the area if the data center is approved. Local workers would gain steady paychecks, but nearby towns would also face new demands on roads, water, and electricity. The endorsement raises the stakes for communities weighing the trade-offs in mid-Michigan.
stories.source_title: Carpenters' union backs STREAM Data Center project - TheDailyNewsOnline.com

stories.title: Haring Township set to halt all data center proposals for six months
stories.dek: Haring Township supervisors will vote on a six-month moratorium that would block new data center applications while the township writes permanent rules. Residents in this Wexford County community worry about sudden spikes in power demand and noise from hyperscale facilities. The pause gives local officials time to study water use and tax impacts before any project advances.
stories.source_title: Haring Township to vote on six-month data center moratorium - 9and10News.com

stories.title: Dingell probes U-M data center plan with Los Alamos ties
stories.dek: Rep. Debbie Dingell released letters from University of Michigan and Los Alamos leaders after raising concerns about a proposed Michigan data center. The exchange highlights local worries over power demand, water use, and university involvement in a hyperscale project. Michigan residents near potential sites could see higher utility bills and strained infrastructure if the facility moves forward.
stories.source_title: Dingell Releases Responses from U-M, Los Alamos National Laboratory Leadership following Inquiry Over Concerns on Proposed Data Center - Representative Debbie Dingell (.gov)

stories.title: Ratepayers rally as Michigan activists warn AI facilities drain power and water
stories.dek: City residents will rally July 18 with more than 50 other communities worried about power and water demands from AI facilities. Kent County activists say the rapid buildout threatens ratepayers and local resources without enough oversight. The event shows Michigan's data center debate is now reaching the streets.
stories.source_title: Grand Rapids Joins 50+ Cities In A July 18 Protest Over AI Data Centers - Mix 95.7

stories.title: Mason residents accuse outsiders of faking support in data center fight
stories.dek: A local debate over a proposed data center in Mason has turned heated after claims that fake grassroots support is being manufactured. Ingham County neighbors worry about noise, power strain and lost farmland if the project advances. The accusations are fracturing trust just as officials weigh next steps.
stories.source_title: Tensions boil over amid astroturfing claim in Mason data center debate - WKAR

stories.title: GOP governor candidates back tax breaks for data centers in Grand Rapids debate
stories.dek: All four Republican candidates for governor said they support tax incentives to attract data centers during a debate in Grand Rapids. They argued the projects would bring construction jobs and long-term property tax revenue to struggling rural townships. Voters heard little detail on how the facilities would affect household electric rates or local water supplies.
stories.source_title: GOP gubernatorial candidates trade personal blows, agree on taxes and data centers - Civic Media

stories.title: Monroe expert warns data-center load could spike bills
stories.dek: A local utility analyst told Monroe officials that unchecked data-center growth will require new power plants and transmission upgrades. Those costs typically flow straight to residential ratepayers through DTE and Consumers Energy filings. The warning comes as several large projects eye southeast Michigan sites.
stories.source_title: Expert: Policies needed to manage data center demand - The Monroe Times

stories.title: Meta's giant Wyoming data center clears early city hurdles
stories.dek: Officials in Wyoming have signed off on the 715,000-square-foot Meta campus, locking in one of the largest projects yet in Kent County. The facility will pull substantial electricity and could reshape local tax rolls and grid planning. Neighbors are already asking what the power draw will mean for their bills.
stories.source_title: Wyoming officials say Meta’s 715,000-square-foot data… - inkl

national.title: Data centers advance rapidly as regulators scramble for rules
national.dek: Across the U.S., officials race to impose guardrails on power-hungry facilities while communities push back on incentives and impacts.

meetings.topic: Vote on 12-year, 50% tax abatement for Oracle America Cloud Services LLC tied to the Related Digital AI data center project; public hearing on Industrial Facilities Exemption Certificate
